summary: Decompiled a chunk of src/melee/ft/ft_0892.c, adding full matches for ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, and fn_8008998C, plus near-complete implementations for ft_800895E0 and ft_80089B08. The PR also typed supporting fighter data by adding IKState, a forward declaration, a typed fn_8008998C prototype, and missing x2085 fighter bitfields. decomp.dev reported +1160 matched code bytes, +16 matched data bytes, and improved ft_80089B08 to 97.81% and ft_800895E0 to 98.86%. No PR body or human review feedback was present in the slice.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: implemented ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, ft_800895E0, fn_8008998C, and ft_80089B08; added needed item, lb, mp, MSL, and JObj includes plus a local ftData_x58_t layout.
Added x2085_b0 through x2085_b7 bitfields in the fighter x2074 area and introduced struct IKState with JObj pointers, Vec3 positions, lengths, and angle limits.
Replaced the unknown fn_8008998C declaration with a typed prototype: s32 fn_8008998C(Fighter*, IKState*, Vec3*).
Added a forward typedef for IKState so headers can expose the typed helper prototype.

what_changed: Implemented ft_800892D4 as a reset/initialization routine for Fighter damage/bonus state and x2070/x2074 fields, including clearing many x2084/x2085 bitfields and x18d8 damage subfields.

Implemented ft_80089460 to clear x2074 vector/bitfield state, store the fighter percent into x2074_vec.x as an integer, and set state flags based on is_metal, x197C, x221D_b6, x2226_b4, x2220_b5, and x2220_b6.

Added a near-matching ft_800895E0 that treats arg1 through union Struct2070, refreshes x2088 via plAttack_80037B08 when the message/state id changes, handles a Luigi x2073 == 0x71 special case, handles an item_gobj/it_8026B6C8 case for x2073 == 0x62, then rebuilds x2074 flags.

Implemented fn_8008998C as a matched helper that queries floor height/normal through mpLib_8004DD90_Floor or mpFloorGetLeft/mpFloorGetRight, clamps vertical adjustment by a 0.45 slope cap, updates ik->pos4.y, and returns whether an adjustment was applied.

Added a near-complete ft_80089B08 for grounded fighter pose/IK handling: it gates on x2219_b5, GA_Ground, db_804D4AF8, and x221C_u16_y bits; builds IKState from ft_data->x58 bone indices and lengths; calls lbVector, lbBgFlash, and JObj helpers; restores saved joint rotations; and applies floor-angle-based ftPartSetRotX with small-line neighbor normal handling.
Added local ftData_x58_t with offset-commented fields used to index fighter parts and limb length data from fp->ft_data->x58.
Extended Fighter x2074 bitfield coverage with x2085_b0 through x2085_b7, enabling direct assignments such as x2085_b3 and x2085_b4.
Updated headers so IKState is forward-declared and fn_8008998C is no longer UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
smart_moves: Used a small local ftData_x58_t with explicit offsets to unblock matching of ft_80089B08 without pretending the whole ft_data->x58 payload is fully understood.

Promoted IKState into types.h and forward.h instead of keeping it hidden locally, because fn_8008998C now has a typed public prototype in ft_0892.h and lbBgFlash helpers operate on the same structure.
Modeled the second byte of x2074 flags as x2085_b0 through x2085_b7, which let the decomp assign individual bits instead of relying on opaque byte stores.
Kept the TODO on ft_800895E0's arg1 type, explicitly noting uncertainty about whether it is just Struct2070; this preserves evidence without over-naming an uncertain interface.
Used union Struct2070 temporaries and pointer reinterpret casts for x2070, UnkPlBonusBits, and Vec2/f32 storage to reproduce original store shapes closely enough to match several functions.
Duplicated the left/right IK setup paths in ft_80089B08 rather than abstracting them, preserving stack/local ordering and helping keep the function near-matching.
Saved and restored JObj rotation quaternions around the IK adjustment calls, matching the apparent original side-effect pattern while still using typed HSD_JObj fields.
decomp_lessons: For fighter bitfields, adding the missing named bits to shared types.h can be necessary before seemingly simple reset functions will match cleanly.
When exposing a newly understood helper signature in a header, add the forward typedef first; this PR introduced IKState in forward.h before using IKState* in ft_0892.h.
Offset-commented provisional structs are useful for progress, but names like ftData_x58_t and fields x0/x4/xC/x18 should be revisited when the asset/data layout becomes clearer.
Grounded IK and floor-adjustment code tends to depend on several subsystems at once: HSD_JObj, lbVector, lbBgFlash, mpLib/mpFloor/mpLine, MSL trig/math, and fighter common data.
Near matches still provide value in this project: decomp.dev credited ft_80089B08 at 97.81% and ft_800895E0 at 98.86%, while three neighboring functions became full matches.

Be careful with MWCC bitfield and stack behavior; this file already documents reversed bitfield order for big-endian local bitfields, and the new code uses padding locals and PAD_STACK to steer stack layout.
Do not over-semantically rename unknown packed states too early; the PR retains Struct2070-style naming and a TODO for ft_800895E0's argument.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names remain in use for this ft_0892 area: ft_800892D4, ft_80089460, ft_800895E0, fn_8008998C, and ft_80089B08.
Unknown fighter fields use offset/bit naming such as x2070, x2073, x2074_vec, x2084_b1, x2085_b3, x221C_u16_y, and x2219_b5.
The newly added IKState has semi-semantic field names for repeated elements: jobj0/jobj1/jobj2, pos0 through pos4, len0/len1, angle_max, and angle_limit.
The local ftData_x58_t follows project-style offset placeholders: x0, x1, x4, x8, x9, xC, x10, x11, x18, with pad_02, pad_0A, and pad_12.
The code keeps existing decomp placeholder style for uncertain types, including union Struct2070 and the TODO questioning whether ft_800895E0 arg1 should be Struct2070.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used union Struct2070 stack temporaries spC, sp18, and sp14 to force exact packed-state assignments and special-case constants such as 0x240063 and 0x44003D.
Used pointer casts like *(s32*) &fp->x2074.x2074_vec.x and *(f32*) &fp->x2074.x207C.x to match bitwise stores into fields typed as vectors/floats.
Inserted explicit unnamed u8 padding arrays in fn_8008998C and ft_80089B08 and ended ft_80089B08 with PAD_STACK(16), indicating stack-shape matching pressure.
Kept literal float constants visible for matching, including 0.0001f, 0.45f, M_PI_2_F, 1.2217305f, 0.34906584f, 0.17453292f, and 0.017453292f.
Used inner scopes with local temporaries such as len, diff, and max_angle to influence lifetime/order and preserve closer compiler output.

Relied on existing library calls instead of open-coding math and collision behavior: mpLib_8004DD90_Floor, mpFloorGetLeft/Right, mpLineGetV0Pos/V1Pos, mpLineGetNormal, lbVector_Diff, lbVector_Normalize, lbVector_Add, lbBgFlash_80021410, and lbBgFlash_80020E38.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the PR slice.
decomp-dev bot reported overall matched code at 63.09%, up +0.03% / +1160 bytes, and matched data at 38.13%, up +16 bytes.
decomp-dev bot reported 4 new matches: ft_800892D4 (+396 bytes), ft_80089460 (+384 bytes), fn_8008998C (+380 bytes), and .sdata (+16 bytes).
decomp-dev bot reported 3 improved unmatched items: ft_80089B08 (+1674 bytes, 97.81%), ft_800895E0 (+387 bytes, 98.86%), and .sdata2 (+56 bytes, 81.82%).
follow_up_queries: ftData_x58_t fighter ft_data x58 bone index layout
